title: Friday Film
description: HamnetThis lush, emotional historical drama—winner of the Golden Globe for Best Drama—is based on the critically acclaimed novel by Maggie O'Farrell, who cowrote the screenplay with Oscar-winning director Chloé Zhao. Set in 16th-century Tudor England, the film follows Agnes, a gifted healer, and her husband Will, whose marriage is pushed to the breaking point by the sudden death of their 11-year-old son, Hamnet, from the plague. Agnes grapples with a raw grief that threatens to consume her, while Will ultimately pours his profound sorrow into the creation of his immortal masterpiece, Hamlet.Starring Jessie Buckley, Paul Mescal, Emily Watson, and Joe Alwyn. Directed by Chloé Zhao. Hamnet received eight Academy Award nominations, including Best Picture, Director, Actress (Buckley), and Adapted Screenplay. 2025 : 126 minutes : Rated PG-13 for thematic content, some strong sexuality, and partial nudity : Historical dramaFree tickets will be given to those who attend.Watch a trailer for Hamnet.Find Hamnet in the library catalog.

title: Visit to Ft Sheridan (Ride Leader Needed)
description:
Status: RIDE LEADER NEEDED

Pace: Moderate

Distance: 30 Miles

Leader: Ride Leader Needed

# !https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/Ft%20Sheridan%20Cavalry%20Training.jpgWe will visit a few interesting locations inside Fort Sheridan

1\. There was an airfield at the fort used during WWII. We will visit its location on the ride.

2\. Anti-aircraft training included firing at drones over the lake. The anti-aircraft gun on display was actually designed and manufactured in Sweden.

3\. Training for the mounted Cavalry was a major activity at the fort until WWII. We will circle the "stable park" shown on the google map. Attached is a horsemanship photo from one of the displays. I found it both frightening and entertaining.

4\. There is a private home on the lake. It has a private road with electric gates.

Lunch will be at Once Upon a Bagel in downtown Highland Park. We will often ride paths rather than roads within the fort to get a different view. The pace will be on the low side of moderate. Part of the ride will be along the lakefront in the Southeast corner of the fort. We will have to walk our bikes over a short grass path.

Today, he is joined by a trio of talented instrumentalists.Free tickets will be given to those present starting at 2:30 pm.

description: Between the Lines meets for online discussions via Zoom. Registered patrons will receive an email the day before the program with the meeting ID and password for joining the event. New members are warmly welcome!Titles are always available on Hoopla, as well as other library ebook platforms. Contact us for assistance, or find any instructions you may need on our Download & Stream page.Book Synopsis: In this stunning novel, Pulitzer Prize and National Book Award–winning author Louise Erdrich tells a story of love, natural forces, spiritual yearnings, and the tragic impact of uncontrollable circumstances on ordinary people’s lives.In the Red River Valley of North Dakota, several lives revolve around a wedding fraught with desire, jealousy, and uncertainty. Gary Geist, a terrified young man set to inherit two farms, is desperate to marry Kismet Poe, an impulsive, lapsed goth who can’t read her own future but will settle for fulfilling his. Her best friend, Hugo, a gentle, red-haired, homeschooled giant, also loves Kismet and is determined to steal her away and build a life together. Kismet’s mother, Crystal, drives a truck for Gary’s family, and on her nightly runs, tunes in to the darkness of late-night radio, experiences visions of guardian angels, and worries about what’s to come, for her daughter and herself.The Mighty Red is Louise Erdrich at her consummate best. A novel of tender humor, disquietude, yearning, community, and family, it is about ordinary people who dream, grow up, fall in love, struggle, endure tragedy, carry bitter secrets; men and women both complicated and contradictory, flawed and decent, lonely and hopeful. Human time, deep time, Red River time, and geological time are explored alongside the impact of crises in our own time—climate change, the depletion of natural resources, the economic meltdown of 2008. It is a story about our tattered bond with the earth, and about love in all of its absurdity and splendor.

title: Fantasy Book Club
description: This month we'll be discussing Daughter of the Moon Goddess by Sue Lynn Tan. Growing up on the moon, Xingyin is unaware that her mother is hiding her from the Celestial Emperor. While fleeing, she meets the Crown Prince, who invites her to train alongside him in archery and magic.This fantasy book is inspired by the legend of the Chinese moon goddess, Chang'e, and is the first in the Celestial Kingdom duology.Find Daughter of the Moon Goddess in the library catalog.

description: Elio Eleven-year-old Elio believes that extraterrestrials are out there and feels he’d fit in better with them than with people. One day, his dream comes true. He's taken to the Communiverse and mistaken for an ambassador from Earth. This fun action-packed cosmic misadventure explores themes of loneliness, belonging, grief, loss, family, acceptance, self-discovery, and identity. Elio and his space-alien best friend Glordon are endearing and relatable, and the animation is bright and imaginative. Featuring the voice talents of Yonas Kibreab, Remy Edgerly, Zoe Saldana, Brad Garrett, Jameela Jamil, Shirley Henderson, and Matthias Schweighofer. Directed by Madeline Sharafian, Domee Shi, and Adrian Molina. title: Pages Through History
description: This month we'll be discussing The Second Founding: How the Civil War and Reconstruction Remade the Constitution by Eric Foner. It would be hard for a person contemplating the United States of 1865 not to conclude that the system the Founders had bequeathed the nation just under a century earlier had been catastrophically flawed. Pulitzer Prize-winning historian Eric Foner lays out the ways the 13th, 14th, and 15th Amendments–all passed in just five years–represented an attempt to fix those flaws. They abolished slavery, codified the principles of birthright citizenship and legal equality, and guaranteed the right of Black men to vote. We live in the America the “Reconstruction Amendments” made, but we’ve been fighting about those amendments ever since.   Find The Second Founding in the library catalog.

title: Umbrella Arts Festival
location: Fountain Square, Downtown Evanston,1601 Sherman Avenue, Evanston, IL 60201, USA
description:
The Umbrella Arts Festival is a celebration of Asian American history, culture and art. The umbrella symbolizes the Asian American diaspora which includes a diversity of ethnicities from over 40 Asian, South Asian, Southeast Asian and Pacific Island countries around the world, and is inclusive of the many intersections of identities that exist in our communities.

Join us for cultural performances, food representing many different Asian cuisines, hands-on art and craft activities, and visit over 60 artists and artisan booths. The festival is free and for all ages!

description: We Bought a ZooBenjamin Mee, recently widowed with two children—14-year-old Dylan and 7-year-old Rosie—thinks that starting over will do them all good, especially after Dylan gets expelled from school. While looking to relocate, they find a dilapidated old house that happens to come with a zoo that needs saving. There, they meet zookeeper Kelly Foster and her small, dedicated staff, who want to see it reopened. Despite the huge financial commitment and having no experience, Benjamin takes on the task with the help of his family.Loosely inspired by a true story, this is a healing adventure about second chances. description: Queen of GloryWriter-director-actor Nana Mensah does triple duty in this film. Sarah returns to the Bronx to settle her affairs, which include inheriting a Christian bookstore called King of Glory, and she finds herself navigating the expectations of Ghanaian relatives and the community while figuring out what she wants and needs. Starring Nana Mensah, Meeko Gattuso, Oberon K.A. description:
Status: Confirmed

Pace: Fast, Any

Distance: approx 140 miles

Leader: Scott Schaedel

# !https://www.ebcrides.org/browser/upload/TOMRV.jpgTour Of the Mississippi River Valley (TOMRV)

TOMRV_2026: https://qcbc.org/content.aspx?page_id=22&club_id=505193&module_id=324415 will start from Goose Lake, IA on June 6 with an overnight and return on June 7. The route is approximately 70 miles on both days; optional start in Bettendorf, IA for approximately 100 miles each day. Click on the link above for registration information. RSVP to Scott Schaedel (svschaed2@gmail.com) so that I can add you to a texting list for meeting logistics. Lodging is suggested at a hotel in Clinton, IA for Friday night before the ride start on Saturday. Clinton is about a 30 minute drive to the start of the ride. The ride features scenic views of the river valley and supported rest stops as well as baggage transport to the Saturday overnight in DuBuque, IA. TOMRV offers hotel information in Bettendorf, but early riders can reserve dormitory room sleeping space at Loras College. Saturday dinner at the college is also included.

Register at: TOMRV_2026: https://qcbc.org/content.aspx?page_id=22&club_id=505193&module_id=324415

title: Time Capsule 2050 Sealing Ceremony
description: Skokie civic leaders will review materials collected from residents and local organizations from the year 2025 and seal them away in our community time capsule to be opened in 2050. Reception will follow. This is a joint project of the Skokie Historical Society and the Skokie Park District, in collaboration with Skokie Public Library. Free tickets will be given to those present starting at 1:30 pm.

title: Evanston Art Center: Summer Camps and Classes
location: Evanston Art Center,1717 Central Street, Evanston, IL 60201, USA
description:
Sign up now for 2026 Summer Camps at the Evanston Art Center! https://www.evanstonartcenter.org/school/dept/summer-youth-camps-and-classes From June 8th to August 13th, we’re offering a variety of mixed-media camps for kids and teens ages 4-18, with flexible half-day and full-day options. Get ready for an unforgettable summer filled with creativity, exploration, and fun! Register through our website or give us a call today!

Have questions? Contact Ellie Hazlett, Director of Education, at ehazlett@evanstonartcenter.org mailto:ehazlett@evanstonartcenter.org mailto:ehazlett@evanstonartcenter.org

description: June 8 is when summer 2026 7-week session classes begin.

title: Nature Play
description: Young children are invited to explore nature in the North Courtyard. Be prepared to dig in and get your hands messy.Drop-in event for children younger than six with an adult.

title: Monarch Community Science Project
description: Monarch butterflies play an important role as pollinators, and their populations are in trouble. You can learn about them and teach others, too.Tend our native garden patch, observe monarch eggs and caterpillars, and talk with other library patrons about your observations and how we can all support monarchs. Data will be submitted to the Monarch Larva Monitoring Project. All participants receive service hours as part of Teen Summer Service. Fill out this contact form to be added to our list of interested teen volunteers and be one of the first to know about volunteer opportunities.Email the teen team to learn more or to express an interest in volunteering. For grade 6-12.

title: Sensory-Friendly Family Movie
description: Strange WorldThis screening is designed for everyone, including families with neurodivergent children and others who may benefit from a less sensory-intense movie experience. We will keep the lights on and turn the sound down so you can shout, sing, or move about however you need to enjoy the film.In Strange World, a family of explorers deal with family conflicts while also adventuring into uncharted lands.2022 : 102 minutes : Rated PG for action/peril and some thematic elements : Animation, Adventure, Sci-FiFree tickets will be given to all who attend. Children younger than eight must be accompanied by an adult.Watch the trailer for Strange World.Find Strange World in the library catalog.

title: Rainbow Family Club
description: Kids in preK-grade 5 and caregivers who are part of an LGBTQ+ family are invited to join our Rainbow Family Club. We'll hang out, chat, have snacks, and do activities. LGBTQ+ kids are also welcome to bring friends who are allies. Each person attending must register.

title: Food For Thought Cookbook Book Club: Salad Days
description: Linger: Salads, Sweets and Stories to Savor, by Hetty Lui McKinnon The Salad Lab: Whisk, Toss, Enjoy! Recipes for Making Fabulous Salads Every Day, by Darlene Schrijver

title: Music Monday
description: Explore how the LGBTQIA+ community helped shape modern music. We'll journey from disco to the birth of house music in Chicago and explore how underground queer culture inspired the beats and anthems that eventually conquered the global charts.Each Music Monday event includes a presentation related to music history, followed by discussion.

title: Middlefork Savanna (Neal Ney)
description:
Status: Confirmed

Pace: Moderate

Distance: 45 Miles

Leader: Neal Ney

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/RWGPS-route.png: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/27865884

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/MiddleforkSavanna.jpgThis week's Tuesday Moderate Ride: A savanna is open woodland with grasses and forbs growing under the trees. We will ride at a moderate pace to this rare remnant from Illinois' pre-settlement past. We'll ride a bit more than four miles on the limestone trail in the preserve at a slow pace with some stops for nature gawking. The trail is suitable for road bikes (as long as you are not in a hurry). After lunch at the Panera on 176, we will resume our moderate pace on the roads and paved paths back to Evanston.

title: Low Vision Group
description: Meet others and learn about current research, assistive devices, and community resources for people who have been diagnosed with an eye condition or have a family member who has vision impairment.This group is facilitated by staff from Friedman Place, a nonprofit organization that provides housing and supportive services to adults who are blind or visually impaired.Keywords: Health & Wellness

title: Summer Reading Party
description: Grab a book and join our reading party. We'll provide snacks and recommendations for your summer reading.Free tickets will be given to all who attend.

title: Library Learning for All Abilities
description: Autistic youth and young people with disabilities age 15-21 are invited to develop job skills and serve the community in a fun and supportive environment. Kind and friendly teens without disabilities who want to develop skills while supporting their peers are also being recruited. Please register for all sessions to participate fully. All participants receive service hours as part of Teen Summer Service. Fill out this contact form to be added to our list of interested teen volunteers and be one of the first to know about volunteer opportunities.Email the teen team to learn more or to express an interest in volunteering.

title: Baby Playtime
description: This drop-in playgroup is for babies age 0 to 23 months. Babies can play with developmentally appropriate toys while their grown-ups socialize with other parents and caregivers.Siblings are welcome. Free tickets will be given to those who attend.

title: Share Your Immigrant Heritage
description: Share your family's immigration story to help celebrate Immigrant Heritage Month. By seeing the history of our community through the forces that brought us into proximity--like opportunity, family ties, or refuge--we can appreciate where we come from, how migration changes us and our communities, and where we might be going. More opportunities to share: Wednesday, June 10, 3-5 pmMonday, June 15, 11 am-1 pm and 4-6 pmFriday, June 26, 11 am-1 pm and 2-4 pm

title: ELL Café
description: Practice speaking English with other members of the community. Volunteer tutors will help with conversations and answer questions. Free tickets will be given to those who attend.Keyword: Adult Learners

title: Crochet Along: Crochet a Summer Sun Hat
description: Enjoy crochet? Interested in learning new stitches or sharing your knowledge? Join us for our virtual crochet-a-long.Each Wednesday in June we will be working on a summer sun hat.  The Make and Do Crew website has a varity of hat patterns to choose from: https://makeanddocrew.com/crochet-sun-hats/ - pick the one that suits you best!Join us each week to share your progress on this or any other projects.

title: Friend Squad
description: Meet new people in a supportive environment and watch your circle of friends grow. Trained peer volunteers host activities to help you get to know others.Autistic youth and young people with developmental and intellectual disabilities are especially welcome. For age 11-15.Contact the teen team to learn more or to express an interest in volunteering.Keywords: ADA35, Teen Volunteering

title: Be You Storytime
description: Be "you-nique," be "be-you-tiful," just be you! Kids age 3-8 and their grown-ups are invited to take pride in their own uniqueness. We'll start in the Storytime Room for stories, songs, and fun, then we'll march to the Activity Room for a one-of-a-kind art project.This event is also being held Tuesday, June 16 at 4:30 pm.Space is limited. Children must be accompanied by an adult. Free tickets will be given to those present starting at 6 pm.

title: Board of Trustees
description: Monthly meetings of the library's Board of Trustees are are open to the public.Board meetings are livestreamed on the library's YouTube Events channel. The upcoming meeting agendas and policy on public comment at board meetings are posted on the library website.

title: LitLounge
description: This month we'll be discussing The Pairing by Casey McQuiston.This romantic comedy finds two exes who reunite on a food tour of Europe and attempt to prove to each other—and themselves—that they’ve moved on. Presented in partnership with Morton Grove Public Library. Find The Pairing in the library catalog.

title: Citizenship Interview Practice and Immigration Information
description: Drop in for one-on-one practice to help you prepare for your citizenship interview.During the citizenship interview, an immigration officer will ask you about your application and background. You will also need to take an English and civics test.Please bring your completed N-400 application if you have one. Stop by HIAS Immigration & Citizenship’s information table, where you can learn about resources and ask questions on a wide range of immigration topics.All information shared will be confidential.

title: Introduction to Yoga
description: Jackie Jasperson leads a live yoga class and shares information about yoga practice. Wear comfortable clothes and bring a mat or towel.Keyword: Health & Wellness

title: Computer Learning Lab
description: Welcome to our self-guided space for adults interested in building their computer skills and knowledge. Build confidence using a library laptop (or bring your own) with a selection of introductory resources that can help you meet your tech goals. Our staff is on hand to guide you through, and you can stay for as long or as little as you need.

description: Learn about the history of Palestinian embroidery, try it for yourself with help from an experienced artist, and take home your own piece.For grade 6–12.

title: Breezy Books and Boba
description: Explore some of the books on our Breezy Reads list through read-alouds and activities and enjoy a variety of flavors of bubble tea and boba. For grade K-2. Kids younger than eight must have an adult in the library during the event.  Allergy notice: Drinks may contain common allergens or ingredients such as dairy, soy, or artificial flavorings. Ingredient information will be available.

title: Cool Story
description: Chat about some of the Cool Reads books and then dig into your artistic side with creative writing exercises. Kids in grade 3-6 attend on their own.

title: 5800 North Café: Monthly Moderate Breakfast Club Ride (Sharon Kaminecki)
description:
Status: Confirmed

Pace: Moderate

Distance: Approx 20 miles

Leader: Sharon Kaminecki

## Monthly Moderate Breakfast Club Ride:

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/5800%20North%20Caf%C3%A9.jpg: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/27045735If you like to eat, ride a bike, meet new people, explore different parts of the city but dedicate only half a day doing it at a time, this service of group bicycle rides is for you! The “Breakfast Club” will ride at a moderate pace to a different neighborhood stop for breakfast at a pre-selected restaurant, and be back around noon

Join us as we ride to breakfast at **5800 North Café**, 5800 N Milwaukee Ave, Chicago in the Jefferson Park neighborhood. Known for its homemade dishes, the cafe offers a delicious selection of pancakes, omelets, fresh hash browns, corned beef hash, and other brunch favorites.

title: Google Slides
description: Similar to Microsoft PowerPoint, Google Slides is a free online application that lets you make and format presentations. This hands-on class focuses on how to use different layouts, themes, backgrounds, and transitions, as well as how to save, download, upload, and print. Bring your own device or use a library Windows laptop. Basic mouse and keyboard skills required.

It goes north of Wauconda, then over to the Broken Oar Marina Bar & Grill on the Fox River in Port Barrington.

title: Garrison Site in Lake Forest (Neal Ney)
description:
Status: Confirmed

Pace: Moderate

Distance: 40 miles

Leader: Neal Ney

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/Garrison%20Site%20Plaque.jpg
!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/RWGPS-route.png: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/36054459 A ride to an important but little known archeological site. It is marked today by only a simple plaque on a bolder, but it is neat to know that there were human settlements 10,000 years ago in the midst of the communities we regularly ride through.

The Garrison Site is an archaeological site in Lake Forest that was excavated in 1991-1992. It is located north of Westleigh Road near Wallace Road and the Carroll Meadow Nature Preserve and was excavated when a new road was put in by developer Barry Carroll.

The Garrison site yielded some of the oldest and best documented evidence of human occupation in Northeast Illinois. Archaeological evidence from the site shows that Paleo Indians were present in Lake Forest as long as 10,000 years ago, though most items found date to the Woodland Period. The site was named in honor of the late Morrow Garrison who had worked closely with Barry Carroll on various developments.

title: Songs of the Illinois Freedom Road
description: Award-winning folk musician and folklorist Chris Vallillo brings the music and the stories of the Underground Railroad in Illinois to life in this engaging and powerful show featuring first-person accounts of Freedom Seekers as well as performances and insights into the music that drove this historic movement. “While traditional accounts of the underground railroad generally dismiss Illinois, our state had more miles bordering slave states than any other” said Chris, “and those borders were rivers which made for excellent escape routes.”Chris Vallillo is a singer/songwriter and roots musician who makes the people and places of “unmetropolitan” America come to life in song. Having spent the last 30 years in the rural Midwest, he has a natural affinity for American roots music. He weaves original, contemporary, and traditional songs and narratives into a compelling and entertaining portrait of the history and lifestyles of the Midwest.Free tickets will be distributed to those present at 2:30 pm.

title: Tour of Columbus Park (Rebecca Tung)
description:
Status: Confirmed

Pace: Casual

Distance: 20-25 miles

Leader: Rebecca Tung

## !https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/Columbus%20Park%20Chicago.jpgThis week's Thursday Casual Ride: Tour of Columbus Park

Unlike Chicago's west parklands, Columbus Park was created entirely by landscape architect Jens Jensen, who designed it between 1915 and 1920. Representing the culmination of his ideas, this 135-acre Prairie Style landscape seven miles west of downtown is considered his public masterpiece.

Columbus Park's design includes a meandering lagoon intended to emulate a "prairie river," complete with cascades and gentle waterfalls constructed of stratified stone. Jensen integrated a pre-existing nine-hole golf course into his plan and used native plants throughout, including hawthorn, oak, and linden trees. The meadow is flanked by a pair of clearings, framed by native vegetation, intended to serve as backstage dressing rooms. Columbus Park is the only park in Chicago designed by Jensen with his signature “council ring,” a circular stone bench for people to come together as equals to share ideas and experiences. Located on the lagoon's northwestern shore, a Mediterranean-revival style refectory, designed by Chatten and Hammond, was added in 1922.

title: Rohingya Music in Exile--Courage, Faith, and Love
description: The Crossing Borders Music String Quartet shares brand-new arrangements of Rohingya music and its stories of courage, faith, and love in exile. Developed after a month of field work in the Rohingya refugee camps of Cox's Bazar, Bangladesh-- the world's largest--this performance shares the melodies, hope, and humanity of Rohingya musicians who are the keepers of their people’s stories and cultural legacy. Transcend physical distance with music that helps everyone "see the Rohingya with our hearts" and celebrates our shared humanity. Free tickets will be distributed to those present at 2:30 pm.

title: Monarch Community Science Project
description: Monarch butterflies play an important role as pollinators, and their populations are in trouble. Teen volunteers will learn about them and teach others, too.Tend our native garden patch, observe monarch eggs and caterpillars, and talk with other library patrons about your observations and how we can all support monarchs. Data will be submitted to the Monarch Larva Monitoring Project. All participants receive service hours as part of Teen Summer Service. All participants receive service hours as part of Teen Summer Service. Fill out this contact form to be added to our list of interested teen volunteers and be one of the first to know about volunteer opportunities.Email the teen team to learn more or to express an interest in volunteering. For grade 6-12.

location: Gichigamiin Indigenous Nations Museum, 3001 Central Street, Evanston, IL, 60201, United States
description:
Come join us for story time!
We will start each session with board books for toddlers, followed by a little movement and music. All of our books are written by Native authors.
The program is FREE for participants. We are excited to see you there!

title: Road Trip Across the USA
description: Celebrate USA's 250th birthday with a fun, hands-on journey across the country. We'll explore the 50 states through stories, games, and movement activities. Travel from region to region and collect stamps along the way—no suitcase required! Free tickets will be given to all who attend. For grade K-2. Kids younger than eight must have an adult in the library during the event.   Keyword: USA 250

title: Teen STEAM Team
description: Design STEAM challenges that will allow kids to experiment and compete. In this session, collaborate with other teen volunteers to mess around with the materials and learn some hands-on teaching methods.Then, on Wednesday, June 17, help lead kids in grade 3-5 in STEAM activities. Finally, apply what you learned with another project, meeting on June 30 and July 1. Volunteers must commit to all four dates.  All participants receive service hours as part of Teen Summer Service. Fill out this contact form to be added to our list of interested teen volunteers and be one of the first to know about volunteer opportunities.Email the teen team to learn more or to express an interest in volunteering. For grade 9-12.

title: The Constitution Today
description: Political analyst Paul Lisnek moderates a nonpartisan panel discussion with Chicagoland university professors. They'll discuss current issues around the First Amendment, power structure of the federal branches, and recent cases heard by the Supreme Court.About the Moderator: Paul Lisnek served as the political and legal analyst for WGN-TV for 18 years. Paul has been a lawyer for 40 years, is a former assistant dean and professor at Loyola Law School, and taught at DePaul University, University of Illinois in Urbana, and Pepperdine University. He teaches constitutional law and ethics for the lawyers bar exam. He was a disciplinary commissioner for lawyers for 25 years, and he served as a legal and jury expert for NBC News and CNN. PanelistsSteven D. Schwinn teaches constitutional law, First Amendment, comparative constitutional law and human rights, and lawyering skills for civil rights at UIC Law.Robert E. Shapiro is a trial lawyer and litigator at Barack Ferrazzano Kirschbaum & Nagelberg LLP with more than 40 years of experience. He handles commercial disputes in trial and appellate courts, ADR forums, and administrative and governmental proceedings nationwide and abroad. He is an adjunct professor of philosophy at Saint Xavier UniversityThis event is part of the Civic Empowerment Series 2026, in partnership with Schaumburg Township District Library, Vernon Area Public Library, Blue Island Public Library, Highland Park Public Library, Aurora Public Library District, La Grange Public Library, Fox River Valley Public Library, Algonquin Area Public Library District, and Oak Park Public Library. Register and receive a Zoom link for this event.For questions about this event, call Schaumburg Township District Library at 847-923-3347.

title: Wilmette Fireworks Dinner Ride (Will Heelan)
description:
Status: Confirmed

Pace: Moderate

Distance: 25 Miles

Leader: Will Heelan

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/Fireworks.jpg!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/RWGPS-route.png: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/43548306EBC Fireworks Enthusiasts: Wilmette will hold its annual Independence Day Fireworks Display on July 3rd at 9:30pm at the lakefront in Gillson Park and we are planning to be there! This moderate ride will **start at Panera Wilmette at 6 pm** to take a 20 mile scenic loop north, returning to Panera in Wilmette around 8 pm. After picking up our carry-out dinners we will proceed to the eastern lawn of the Baha'i Temple (just off the west public sidewalk on Sheridan Road south of the Wilmette Harbor bridge and about 90 feet north of Linden Avenue). There we will stake out our viewing location and enjoy our picnic while we wait for the fireworks to start. Feel free to join us for any or all of these activities. This ride is a great way to celebrate the Independence Day holiday! **Lights and helmets mandatory**, something to sit on highly recommended, (also consider bug repellant if you are susceptible).

title: Barrington Moretti's (Will Heelan)
description:
Status: Confirmed

Pace: Fast

Distance: 50 Miles

Leader: Will Heelan

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/RWGPS-route.png: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/39695420

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/Barrington%20Hills.jpgStart: Village Green Park in Northbrook (corner of Walters & Meadow) at 9 am. If you drive to the start, the streets may have parking restrictions if there is a festival in the park. There is a parking lot on Walters just southeast of the park where you should be able to park.

We will ride through Long Grove and the rolling hills of Barrington Hills, passing horse farms and mansions. Then our route drops down to our lunch stop at the Moretti's in Barrington. After lunch we return on beautiful Old Checker Road before stopping at Culver's for a quick rest/snack. The pace will be Fast (15-17 mph).

We will ride at a moderate pace to this rare remnant from Illinois' pre-settlement past. We'll ride a bit more than four miles on the limestone trail in the preserve at a slow pace with some stops for nature gawking. The trail is suitable for road bikes (as long as you are not in a hurry). After lunch at the Panera on 176, we will resume our moderate pace on the roads and paved paths back to Evanston.

title: Annual EBC Picnic (Mary Fry)
description:
Status: PENDING!

Leader: Mary Fry

## Our annual EBC picnic at Evanston's Lighthouse Park(Central/Sheridan):

The day begins with a morning ride and culminates in a potluck lunch. Check the Rides Calendar to find the perfect ride for you. All rides begin and end at the Lighthouse Beach Park. Please bring a dish to share that does not spoil easily, (no mayonnaise salads, raw meat/there is no grill). Beginning @ 8:00 AM, coolers and a watchful eye will be available for food. The club will provide soda, plates/eating utensils, and veggie and meat roll-ups.

Whether or not you ride, please come to the picnic. We always have a good time! (No alcoholic beverages. That would be a violation of park rules). Questions, suggestions, or volunteer to help, tele/text Mary Fry @ 773-383-2466.

title: Mucho Gusto Barra + Concina: Monthly Moderate Breakfast Club Ride (Sharon Kaminecki)
description:
Status: Confirmed

Pace: Moderate

Distance: Approx 20 miles

Leader: Sharon Kaminecki

## Monthly Moderate Breakfast Club Ride:

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/Mucho%20Gusto%20Barra%20%2B%20Concina.jpg: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/27045735If you like to eat, ride a bike, meet new people, explore different parts of the city but dedicate only half a day doing it at a time, this service of group bicycle rides is for you! The “Breakfast Club” will ride at a moderate pace to a different neighborhood stop for breakfast at a pre-selected restaurant, and be back around noon.

Join us as we amigos roll to breakfast at [](https://joedonut.com/locations/niles/)**Mucho Gusto Barra + Concina**, 2700 N Milwaukee Ave, Chicago, in the Logan Square neighborhood. Inspired by the colors and charm of San Miquel de Allende, the brunch menu includes breakfast burritos, chilaquiles, French toast, and huevos al bajio.

title: Sorelle Italian Market and Café: Monthly Moderate Breakfast Club Ride (Sharon Kaminecki)
description:
Status: Confirmed

Pace: Moderate

Distance: Approx 20 miles

Leader: Sharon Kaminecki

## Monthly Moderate Breakfast Club Ride:

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/Sorelle%20Italian%20Market%20and%20Caf%C3%A9%20-%20Glen.jpg: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/27045735If you like to eat, ride a bike, meet new people, explore different parts of the city but dedicate only half a day doing it at a time, this service of group bicycle rides is for you! The “Breakfast Club” will ride at a moderate pace to a different neighborhood stop for breakfast at a pre-selected restaurant, and be back around noon.

Join us as we ride to breakfast at **Sorelle Italian Market and Café**, 1941 Tower Dr, Glenview. Menu features breakfast croissants, egg bites, and breakfast brioche along with Italian pastries

title: Drum, shake, make music.
description:
The Chicago Drum Circle Meetup Group
• What we'll do
Open, lightly facilitated, family friendly, community drum circle.

• What to bring
Bring yourself, a friend, and a hand drum, percussion instrument or a found sound.

• Important to know
This is a Ridgeville Park District partnership program. Alcohol, tobacco, vaping and drugs are strictly prohibited.

title: Gus's Drive-In, Wisconsin (Neal Ney)
description:
Status: Confirmed

Pace: Moderate

Distance: 49 Miles

Leader: Neal Ney

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/RWGPS-route.png: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/30880656

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/guss-drive-in-east-troy.jpgI love to ride in the SE Wisconsin countryside near Burlington, and Gus’s Drive-in is a favorite lunch spot. We have had fast and fast plus paced rides in this area for years. A few years back Ken Schulein put together a shorter route suitable for moderate riders that has become one of my favorite routes in this area. The route travels on rural roads with rolling hills to Gus's Drive-in, East Troy, WI for lunch at mile 25. Rest Stops with snacks are at mile 12 and Adrian's Frozen Custard in Burlington at mile 38.

Wisconsin Start: Brighton_Dale_Golf_Course: https://www.google.com/maps/place/Brighton+Dale+Links/@42.6305121%20-88.1179985%2012z/data=!4m2!3m1!1s0x8805790ac45286f9:0x24038a6eaef072f2. Take I-94 North. 7.5 miles past the Wisconsin border take Exit #340 WI-142 West toward Burlington. Turn left (west) at the end of the exit ramp and go west 8.5 miles on WI-142. Turn right (north) at WI-75 (248th St) and go 0.7 miles. Turn left into golf course entrance. Follow the park road about 0.5 mile to the parking lot next to the club house. Park at an empty area of the lot. Allow an hour and 15 minutes from Evanston.

title: Foley Pond (Neal Ney)
description:
Status: Confirmed

Pace: Moderate

Distance: 30 miles

Leader: Neal Ney

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/D5CB3ACE-D71C-4FB6-A5F9-5607FE8675EF_1_201_a.jpg!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/RWGPS-route.png!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/Foley%20Pond%20HP.jpg: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/44025534Tuesday's Moderate Ride: Foley Pond is a hidden gem of a park – essentially a fishing hole – in Highland Park. Most of us would never stumble across it in our usual comings and goings. It’s hard enough to find when you know that it is there. No parking lot, very subtle signage, no playground equipment, and no paved surfaces.

We’ll ride to Lake Forest along Ridge, take Old Elm and a few other streets east and south to Highland Park, stopping to lunch at Once Upon a Bagel. In part this route is an excuse to visit this old EBC favorite and Stanley-approved restaurant, that I don’t think we have visited enough lately. After lunch we’ll pay a short visit to Foley Pond. Fishing and birding are the major activities here, but we won’t be doing either. Just looking and maybe taking a couple of pictures.

title: The Perch Kitchen and Tap: Monthly Moderate Breakfast Club Ride (Sharon Kaminecki)
description:
Status: Confirmed

Pace: Moderate

Distance: Approx 20 miles

Leader: Sharon Kaminecki

## Monthly Moderate Breakfast Club Ride:

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/The%20Perch%20Kitchen%20and%20Tap.jpg: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/27045735If you like to eat, ride a bike, meet new people, explore different parts of the city but dedicate only half a day doing it at a time, this service of group bicycle rides is for you! The “Breakfast Club” will ride at a moderate pace to a different neighborhood stop for breakfast at a pre-selected restaurant, and be back around noon.

Join us as we ride to breakfast at **The Perch Kitchen and Tap**, 1932 W Division, Chicago in the Wicker Park neighborhood. The brunch menu includes omelets, fried egg sandwich, avocado toast, and lemon blueberry pancakes.

title: Family Day 2026
location: Gichigamiin Indigenous Nations Museum, 3001 Central Street, Evanston, IL, 60201, United States
description:
Join us for an afternoon of family crafts, storytelling, shop at our Artists Alley, enjoy live music, and so much more!

Throughout the event you can visit info booths to learn more about other incredible work being done by our partnering organizations. Visit our Artists Alley to shop at a handful of Native artists booths, enjoy live music, and visit our ongoing exhibits. This event will have something for everyone. Limited parking is available along Central Street and the parking lot behind the museum.

Full event details coming soon!

For more information about this program, please contact: info@gichigamiin.org | (847) 475-0911.

title: Indigenous Peoples' Day
location: Gichigamiin Indigenous Nations Museum, 3001 Central Street, Evanston, IL, 60201, United States
description:
Indigenous Peoples’ Day honors the culture and history of Native people past, present, and future. Join us at Gichigamiin Indigenous Nations Museum to celebrate the diversity and vibrancy of Indigenous cultures with FREE admission to the museum all day. Bring your friends and your family to enrich your understanding of Indigenous histories and cultures and enjoy guided craft activities.
Full schedule of events coming soon!

title: Dagwaagin (Fall) Artist Market - Small Business Saturday
location: Gichigamiin Indigenous Nations Museum, 3001 Central Street, Evanston, IL, 60201, United States
description:
Save the Date for our Dagwaagin (Fall) Artist Market – Small Business Saturday
This program honors Native American entrepreneurs and brings together innovative Native artists, business owners, leaders, and creators with community members, buyers, and potential clients. Participating Indigenous artists and entrepreneurs will have the chance to share their arts and crafts, contributions, accomplishments, and professional services with the public, allowing guests to support these Indigenous-owned companies by purchasing items or fostering future business opportunities.
Admission is FREE

Native Artists and Small Businesses
Start your holiday shopping early and show your support for local Native American Small Businesses this Small Business Saturday.

For more information about this program, please contact:
Josee Starr | jstarr@gichigamiin.org | (847) 475-0911

title: Lake Forest College Easter Brunch (Sara Polonsky)
description:
Status: PENDING!

Pace: Moderate

Distance: 32 miles

Leader: Sara Polonsky

# !https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/RWGPS-route.png: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/27000578

!http://myfrugaladventures.com/wp-content/uploads/2012/03/easter-basket.jpg**LEAVING PANERA @ 09:00 AM**. Join us at 9:00 am on Easter Sunday for a Moderate ride (13-15mph) to Lake Forest College for brunch. If the Easter Brunch Fast Ride is too fast for you, but you still want to enjoy the abundant brunch (for only $12.50) join us at Panera in Wilmette @ 9am for the Moderate version! Note: The ride will not happen if it is raining, snowing or otherwise miserable out.
Don't eat breakfast before you leave for this ride! This ride is back by popular demand after being on hiatus since 2019. For those of you who think inflation is now running rampant, **check this out**: Fabulous brunch (Lake Forest College students eat very, very well) - this year for a special deal of only **$12.50** (cash or credit card). (FYI: **2019**-$16.00; **2018**-$11.35; **2017**-$10.31; **2016**-9.92; **2015**-$9.55)

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/Liberty%20Restaurant.jpgThis week's Tuesday Moderate Ride: We will celebrate Independence Day with a ride to Libertyville and lunch at the Liberty restaurant. If there are enough riders interested there will also be a slightly faster section led by Will.
